City Manager 
823-4299
Com m u n ity Developme nt 823-4244
Finance/Personnel 823-4247
0cr 1;
, '31
1225 Lincoln Way
Auburn, Cal ifornia 95603
FAX (916) 885.5508
October 9, 1991-
Mr. Scott Hal-Labrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
I am City Attorney for the City of Auburn and recently spoke with
Mark Morodomi concerning a confLict of interest question. Mr.
Morodomi suggested that I could write and request advice more
forrnally. As set forth below, my question concerns whether
Auburn Councilmember Deborah Yue may participate in certain
upcoming council actions. In reguesting your advice, I have set
forth some of the general background concerning the upcorning
council issues, the anticipated i-ssues which will face the
council, and, finally, 
a brief summary of Ms. Yuers economic
interests.
BACKGROUND
Auburn has for a number of years had flooding and drai-nage
problems in the historic OId Town area. This problem is of major
concern for the City, dS the OId Town area piays a vital role in
the commerce of the City.
The O1d Town drainage basin, a tributary of Auburn Ravine, drains
the southeastern half of the city. 
The major run-off
contributing area lies between I-80 and the Southern Pacific
Railroad and extends south to Skyridge Drive and the Auburn
Recreation park. The total area that drains into the Ol-d Town
area is approximately 1 square miLe or about 16 percent of the
total city area. In these drainage basins there are
approximalely 600 residences in the Lincotn Way basin and 1"25 in
tfrL erewery Lane basin, representing a population of over I,'7OO'
There are two principal drainages that enter the OId Town
commercial/residential area. The larger is the Li-ncoln Way
$y
A
\.Cr


Mr. scott Hallabrin
October 9, L99l
Page 2
drainage from the north and the smaller is the Brewery Lane
drainage to the south. The confluence of the two drainages lies
beneath the boards/a1k in front of the Old To$rn post office at the
intersection of Washington Street and Sacramento Street. 
The
conbined flo$rs are carried in a covered channel and concrete box
culvert under Commercial Street and discharges into an open
channel along I-80.
The problen is that the existing drainage system is grossly
inadeguate. The century-o1d system cannot handle the run-off
from a storm of a ten-year rnagnitude. Current culvert drainaqe
systems in the O1d Tonn area can only handle L50 cfs (Linco]n Way
basin) and 75 cfs (Brer{ery Lane basin). The estirnated peak run-
off into the Lincoln Way and Brer{rery Lane culverts from a 1
percent or 100-year storm is 570 cfs and 170 cfs, respectively.
When this occurs, or even a storn of the 1,0-50 year rnagnitude,
the Old Town area is greatly affected. In 1985 the area flooded
due to a significant ston0, and the danage to both private and
public property was such that the area yras declared a disaster
area by the state. 
Businesses were closed for repairs, and the
Ioss to private property rras substantial. 
The city suffered
major damage, causing street closures in the area. The damage to
the.area disrupted conmerce in the area for weeks. Both
businesses and the City suffered losses in hundreds of thousands
of dollars in terrns of loss of trade, property and cost of
repairs.
The historic O1d Town area j.s extrenely important to the Auburn
cornnunity, acting as the prinary entrance into Auburn. The area
contains nany historic buildings built in the 1850s without the
benefits of today's engineering knowledge, and the loss of these
buildings would cause a rippling effect in the total comrnunity.
It is because of the key role of the old Tohrn area that the City
has placed a high priority on this drainage project.
FININCIAIT INTERE8TB
Deborah Yue is narried to Herbert Yue. Herbert Yue and Deborah
Yue reside at 215 Sacranento street. 
This parcel is located
between sacramento street and Brewery Lane. The Yues own that
property in joint tenancy.
Herbert Yue and Harry Yue own property at 147 Brewery Lane, held
in joint tenancy between thern. There is a small residential
structure on this property occupied by Harry Yue. Herbert Yue
and Deborah Yue receive no rental income fron this property.


Mr. scott HaI]abrin
October 9, 19 91
Page 3
Herbert Yue is president and 25 percent shareholder of RHRH, Inc.
Thj,s is a smalf closely held famj,ly corporation. RHRH, Inc.,
owns a parcel on washi-ngton Street in the Ol-d Toern area, which
property is Leased by century 2l- for real estate offices.
Herbert Yue receives a salary from RHRH, Inc.
RHRH, Inc., leases property at 289 Washington Street, in the OId
Town area, from wi-Ibur Yue. The Shanghai Restaurant is operated
at this site. 
The Shanghai Restaurant has sustained fl-ood damage
in the past from rnajor storms.
UPCOUING ICTIONS
Recently, the city Councj,L approved a pl-an for both short-term
and long-term corrective measures regarding storrn drainage in the
OId Town area. Ms. Yue abstained fron those actions, feeling
that it s/ould be appropriate to do so until a fornal opinion on
conflict of interest issues was rendered. It is anticipated that
the following actions wiII be forthcorning and Ms. Yue would like
to know if it is appropriate for her to participate in these
issues.
1. 
Councifmembers also sit as nernbers of the Auburn Urban
Development Authority. 
On October 2A, L99)-, the AUDA
will consider budgeting funds for portions of the
corrective measures pertaining to stom draj.nage in oId
Town .
2. 
Later this year the City Council will consider a
utility 
user fee ordj.nance, the purpose of which witl
be to help pay for corrective neasures.
3. 
Early next year the Council wilf consider entering into
contracts with munj,cj.paL bond underwrj-ters and bond
counsel j-n connection with a possible bond issue to
help fund corrective neasures.
4. 
Next year the Council will also consider approving a
capital improvernent budget to fund a portion of the
storm drainage project. 
At roughly the same tirne AUDA
wil-L consider approval of its budget, including
budgeting funds for the storm drainage project.
5. 
FinalLy, at some point after the above actions, the
Council wiIl consider approval of an engineering
contract for the project, advertisernent of bids for the
work and consideration of bids for the project,


Mr. scott Hallabrin
october 9, L99L
Page 4
CONCITITAIOII
I realize that section 18702.3(a) (2) of the Regulations of the
Fair Potitical Practices connission provides that the effect of a
decision is nrateriat as to real property if the decision involves
construction of, or improvements to, storm drainage facilities
and the real property 1n which the official 
has an interest will
receive nev, or substantially inproved services. However, any
improvement in the storm drainage facilities 
in the OId Tot n area
will create a substantial benefit to the entire old Town
commercial area and the city as weI1. Please provide whatever
input you can on whether Councilrnember Yue nay participate in the
upconing issues outlined above. Most inmediate, of course, is
tle upconing issue of october 28, !99L, as outlined above. rf at
all possible, advice would be appreciated before that tine.
If you need any further information or clarification 
of the
infornation provided herein, please do not hesitate to contact
Your assistance is greatly appreciated.
l.r"ry yy'"yb,
,t/*[U/,L/L
CHARLES WACHOB
city Attorney
cw\ea

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Auburn Councilmember Deborah Yue may participate in upcoming city council actions regarding storm drainage improvements in the Old Town area, given her and her husband's financial interests in property that would be affected by the drainage project.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The letter discusses background, financial interests, and upcoming actions, then concludes with a request for FPPC input. Document references Section 18702.3(a)(2) of FPPC regulations. Some OCR artifacts present but content is legible.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Auburn Councilmember Deborah Yue may participate in upcoming city council actions regarding storm drainage improvements in the Old Town area, given her and her husband's financial interests in property that would be affected by the drainage project.

embedding.summary: City Attorney of Auburn requests FPPC advice regarding whether Councilmember Deborah Yue can participate in upcoming council decisions on storm drainage projects in Old Town, given the Yue family's property holdings in the affected area and their business interests.
